title: palicourea angustiflora
intro: Palicourea angustiflora, also known as the 'Brazilian guinea', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Palicourea angustiflora include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antihemorrhagic agent. Herbal preparations of Palicourea angustiflora are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by mixing the leaves with other herbs in a tincture.
